title: Direct To A Fix
disclaimer: Training support only. Confirm local direct routing procedures and coordination requirements with an instructor.
learning_objectives: Assign direct routing to a fix on the aircraft route.
Monitor the aircraft until the direct-to fix is captured.
Confirm that route sequencing continues after the fix.
key_points: Direct-to is a route instruction, not just a heading.
The fix must be known and suitable for the aircraft route.
After capture, the aircraft should continue through the planned route sequence.

lesson_steps.title: Known Fix
lesson_steps.body: A direct-to instruction sends the aircraft toward a named fix. The student should confirm that the fix belongs to the intended route context.

lesson_steps.title: Route Continuation
lesson_steps.body: After the aircraft reaches the direct-to fix, check that it continues toward the next route point.

lesson_steps.title: Direct-To Drill
script: Select an aircraft before a route midpoint.
Assign direct to the midpoint fix.
Monitor the turn and fix capture.

exercise.title: Send One Aircraft Direct To A Route Fix
exercise.objective: Assign a direct-to instruction and observe route sequencing after fix capture.
student_actions: Assign direct to a forward fix.
Monitor until the aircraft captures the fix.
success_conditions: The direct-to fix is valid for the aircraft route.
The aircraft captures the fix and continues route navigation.
debrief_prompts: Was the selected fix ahead of the aircraft?
Did the aircraft continue route navigation after capture?
What coordination might be needed before direct routing?
